data.title: TP Receives Frost & Sullivan's 2026 India Company of the Year Recognition for Excellence in Customer Experience Management Services
data.description: TP, previously known as Teleperformance, has been acknowledged for its prominence in digital transformation, responsible AI integration, and outcome-focused outsourced services. The announcement was made on April 6, 2026, in SAN ANTONIO by Frost & Sullivan. This recognition underscores TP's position as a global leader in digital services.

data.title: Audited annual report 2025
data.description: The consolidated audited financial results for 2025 of AS Merko Ehitus have been confirmed to be the same as the initial disclosure made on 5 February 2026.

data.title: Aktsiaselts Infortar own share acquisition transactions
data.description: During the period from March 30th to April 2nd, 2026, Aktsiaselts Infortar purchased its own shares on the Nasdaq Tallinn Stock Exchange. The acquisition involved a significant number of shares, demonstrating the company's confident investment in itself. This strategic move by Infortar highlights its commitment to growth and stability in the market.

data.title: Broadcom, Vivos Therapeutics And 3 Stocks To Watch Heading Into Monday
data.description: Today, U.S. stock futures are trading with a mixed outlook. One of the stocks drawing attention is Cheer Holding Inc. (NASDAQ: CHR), which recently announced a 1-for-3 reverse stock split effective from April 6 to uphold its Nasdaq listing. Following this news, the shares of Cheer Holding surged by 9.4% to $0.87 in after-hours trading. Another stock in focus is Vivos Therapeutics Inc. (NASDAQ: VVOS) which witnessed a significant increase in its share value after hours on Thursday. This surge came after a Securities and Exchange Commission filing disclosed that a Michigan-based investor group acquired a notable 19.9% stake in the company through a $2.39 million investment. For more detailed information, the complete story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: RPM Earnings Are Imminent; These Most Accurate Analysts Revise Forecasts Ahead Of Earnings Call
data.description: RPM International Inc. (NYSE: RPM) is set to announce its third-quarter earnings before the market opens on April 8. Analysts predict the Ohio-based company will report earnings of 35 cents per share, compared to 35 cents per share in the same period last year. The consensus estimate for RPM's quarterly revenue is $1.55 billion, up from $1.48 billion the previous year. RPM International recently declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of 54 cents per share. Following this news, RPM shares closed at $97.97, a 2.6% decrease. Interested readers can find more details and analyst ratings on Benzinga Pro's Analyst Stock Ratings page. For an in-depth analysis of the company's performance and ratings by Benzinga's most-accurate analysts, visit Benzinga.com.
